tool_args.label: MTA Grand Central Madison Concourse Operating Company
tool_args.raw_text: Creation of New Subsidiary Corporation Pursuant to Public Authorities Law - MTA Grand Central Madison Concourse Operating Company

payload.entity_name: MTA Grand Central Madison Concourse Operating Company
payload.description: New subsidiary created to operate and maintain the new East Side Access terminal and concourse below Grand Central Terminal. Will oversee private sector operator with fewer than 10 staff.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Creation of New Subsidiary Corporation Pursuant to Public Authorities Law
create a new public benefit corporation subsidiary of the Metropolitan Transportation Authority ... for the purpose of operating and maintaining the new East Side Access terminal and concourse
public benefit corporation subsidiary known as MTA Grand Central Madison Concourse Operating Company ("MTA GCMC")

tool_args.label: 42nd Street Shuttle Reconstruction (Times Square Shuttle)
tool_args.raw_text: C&D contractors have rebuilt the Times Square Shuttle, straightened the tracks, and went from three tracks to two tracks which allowed C&D to consolidate the platforms resulting in a much better operation. The longer and wider platforms and trains resulted in a twenty percent increase in capacity.

payload.description: Rebuilt the Times Square Shuttle (42nd Street Shuttle): straightened tracks, reduced from three tracks to two, consolidated platforms, resulting in 20% increase in capacity. Now fully ADA compliant. New connection between 42nd Street-Times Square and Bryant Park Stations added.

evidence_refs.source_quote: over the past twenty months, C&D contractors have rebuilt the Times Square Shuttle, straightened the tracks, and went from three tracks to two tracks which allowed C&D to consolidate the platforms resulting in a much better operation

tool_args.label: Cherry Valley Avenue Bridge Removal and Replacement
tool_args.raw_text: Design-Build Services for the Cherry Valley Road Bridge Removal & Replacement located in Garden City on the Long Island Railroad's Hempstead Branch.

payload.project_name: Cherry Valley Avenue Bridge Removal and Replacement
payload.location: Garden City, NY on LIRR Hempstead Branch between Nassau Boulevard and Garden City Stations
payload.description: Removal and replacement of the Cherry Valley Avenue Bridge, originally constructed in 1906, carrying two active electrified tracks of the Hempstead Branch. Contract awarded to Railroad Construction Company, Inc. for $16,663,538.40 for 720 days.

location_normalized.raw_text: Garden City, NY on LIRR Hempstead Branch between Nassau Boulevard and Garden City Stations

evidence_refs.source_quote: Award of a contract for the removal and replacement of the Cherry Valley Avenue Bridge located in Garden City on the Long Island Railroad's Hempstead Branch. Request Value: $16,663,538.40

The Cherry Valley Road Bridge was originally constructed in 1906 as a two-bay single span bridge supported by three non-redundant girders carrying two active electrified tracks of the Hempstead Branch

tool_args.label: 40 Quay Street Development and NYCT Relocation
tool_args.raw_text: 40 QUAY STREET DEVELOPMENT AND RELATED RELOCATION OF EXISTING NYCT MOBILE WASH AND NYCT MATERIAL STORAGE UNITS

payload.location: 40 Quay Street, Greenpoint, Brooklyn with NYCT relocation to 190 Varick Ave, Brooklyn
payload.description: Development of 80,617sf industrial property on East River waterfront in Greenpoint, Brooklyn. Conditional designation of Gotham Property Acquisition LLC to redevelop via 99-year ground lease. NYCT Mobile Wash and Material Storage Units to relocate. Total value to MTA up to $92,278,023 (with rezoning and ERU facility).

location_normalized.raw_text: 40 Quay Street, Greenpoint, Brooklyn with NYCT relocation to 190 Varick Ave, Brooklyn

evidence_refs.source_quote: AGENCY: MTA New York City Transit ("NYCT")
The Quay Street Property, an 80,617sf ± industrial property, was purchased by NYCT in 1998 for $2.5 million and is located on the East River waterfront in the Greenpoint neighborhood of Brooklyn.
Rezoning ... TOTAL VALUE TO MTA $65,731,062 (without ERU) $92,278,023 (with ERU)

tool_args.label: MTA Grand Central Madison Concourse Operating Company
tool_args.raw_text: Creation of New Subsidiary Corporation Pursuant to Public Authorities Law - MTA Grand Central Madison Concourse Operating Company

payload.entity_name: MTA Grand Central Madison Concourse Operating Company
payload.description: New subsidiary created to operate and maintain the new East Side Access terminal and concourse below Grand Central Terminal. Will oversee private sector operator with fewer than 10 staff.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Staff Summary

To obtain Board approval to: (i) create a new public benefit corporation subsidiary of the Metropolitan Transportation Authority (the "MTA") pursuant to Public Authorities Law Section 1266(5) for the purpose of operating and maintaining the new East Side Access terminal and concourse; and (ii) authorize the Chair, or the Chair's respective designees, to carry out the purposes and functions of this new subsidiary agency.
public benefit corporation subsidiary known as MTA Grand Central Madison Concourse Operating Company ("MTA GCMC")

issues: evidence_refs[1].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer
